scalar: max over premises of bidirectional avg of (P(entail)-P(contradict)+1)/2
runner: run on Mini GPU via the frozen NLIScorer; DO NOT run locally

probes.stale_hypothesis: The delivery exceeds Silo 4's remaining free capacity, so it may not be received into the silo as is.
probes.correct_final_state: 32 vs 35 -> within; 16.5 vs 14 -> violates; 32 vs 20 -> violates
premises: The delivery cannot be received and stored as planned: although the corrected free capacity of 35 tonnes in Silo 4 is now sufficient for the 32-tonne load, the wheat's 16.5% moisture content exceeds the 14% storage cap and must first pass through the dryer, and the 32-tonne load cannot be unloaded within the 1-hour slot given the auger's 20-tonne-per-hour throughput, so both the moisture rule and the yard scheduling policy stop the delivery from proceeding as planned.
Document [4] corrects document [1] by updating Silo 4's free capacity from 25 tonnes to 35 tonnes after an overnight transfer to the export loading bay.
The 25-tonne free capacity figure from document [1] is now superseded and treated as false.
Silo 4's current free capacity is 35 tonnes.
Today's delivery is 32 tonnes of wheat.
Since 32 tonnes is less than or equal to the 35-tonne free capacity, the delivery does not exceed Silo 4's structural fill limit under intake policy.
Storage rules cap incoming wheat moisture content at 14% for long-term storage in Silo 4 without additional drying.
The grower's delivery docket lists a lab-tested moisture content of 16.5%, which exceeds the 14% cap.
Storage rules state wheat above the moisture cap may not be binned without passing through the dryer first, so this delivery cannot be binned directly into Silo 4.
The intake auger serving Silo 4 is rated for a maximum throughput of 20 tonnes per hour.
At 20 tonnes per hour, unloading the full 32-tonne load requires 1.6 hours, exceeding the 1-hour unloading slot.
Yard policy states a delivery may not be booked into a slot shorter than its throughput-determined unloading time, so the 1-hour slot violates yard policy for this 32-tonne load.

Even though the capacity constraint is now satisfied due to the corrected free capacity figure, the moisture content violation and the auger throughput/slot violation remain unresolved and independently block the delivery as planned.
probes.note: score max-over-premises bidirectional NLI of stale_hypothesis; compare to a corrected-sentence hypothesis built from correct_final_state. stale>=corrected => real contamination; corrected>stale => instrument false positive.

probes.stale_hypothesis: The new rack's heat load exceeds Row 6's spare cooling margin, so it may not be energised as planned.
probes.correct_final_state: 18 vs 20 -> within; 23 vs 18 -> violates; 9 vs 15 -> violates
premises: The new rack cannot be brought online in Row 6 as planned; although the corrected 20 kW spare cooling margin now covers the 18 kW heat load, the rack is still blocked by the supply-air temperature at 23°C exceeding the 18°C maximum and the under-floor static pressure at 9 pascals being below the required 15 pascals minimum.
A new rack of 40 blade servers adds a fixed heat load of 18 kW to Row 6.
Document 4 corrects the earlier statement that Row 6's spare cooling margin is 12 kW, raising it to 20 kW after an additional CRAC unit was commissioned.
The original 12 kW spare cooling margin figure from document 1 is now superseded and treated as false.
With the corrected 20 kW spare cooling margin, the 18 kW additional heat load from the new rack does not exceed Row 6's spare cooling capacity margin.
Facilities policy states a rack may not be energised if its heat load exceeds the row's spare cooling margin, and this condition is now satisfied given the corrected 20 kW margin.
The CRAC units serving Row 6 are rated for a maximum supply-air temperature of 18°C.
A sensor fault currently has the CRAC units supplying air at 23°C, which exceeds the rated maximum of 18°C.
Facilities policy states a rack may not be energised while supply-air temperature exceeds the rated maximum, and this condition is currently violated.
Row 6's under-floor plenum requires a minimum static pressure of 15 pascals.
A blocked floor tile has caused the current under-floor static pressure reading to be only 9 pascals, below the required minimum.
Facilities policy states a rack may not be energised while under-floor pressure is below the minimum requirement, and this condition is currently violated.
probes.note: score max-over-premises bidirectional NLI of stale_hypothesis; compare to a corrected-sentence hypothesis built from correct_final_state. stale>=corrected => real contamination; corrected>stale => instrument false positive.

probes.stale_hypothesis: The maize dried by the drying line is 120 tonnes, which fits within the 140 tonnes dryer throughput capacity.
probes.correct_final_state: total2=152 tonnes (corr2 applied) vs capacity 140 tonnes; total1 still 504 tonnes; season's diesel allowance for the drying line still $9,000.
premises: With the corrected yields, the barley harvest is 504 tonnes (falling short of the required 552-tonne contract) and the maize dried is 152 tonnes (exceeding the 140-tonne dryer capacity), so both of these requirements fail, while only the diesel budget requirement ($9,000 against a required minimum of $8,200) is met.
Millbrook Farms is harvesting 120 hectares of barley this season.
The original agronomy report stating barley yields 5.0 tonnes per hectare has been superseded and is treated as false.
The revised agronomy report states barley yields 4.2 tonnes per hectare this season.
Barley harvest is calculated as per-hectare yield multiplied by hectares.
Using the corrected yield, barley harvest equals 4.2 tonnes/hectare x 120 hectares = 504 tonnes.
The contract requires a delivery of 552 tonnes, and delivery below that fails the contract.
504 tonnes is less than the required 552 tonnes, so the barley harvest requirement fails.
The drying line has 40 bins.
The original drying-line report stating each bin dries 3.0 tonnes of maize has been superseded and is treated as false.
The revised drying-line report states each bin dries 3.8 tonnes of maize per bin this run.
Maize dried is calculated as per-bin yield multiplied by number of bins.
Using the corrected rate, maize dried equals 3.8 tonnes/bin x 40 bins = 152 tonnes.
Dryer throughput capacity is capped at 140 tonnes.
152 tonnes exceeds the 140-tonne capacity cap, so the maize drying requirement fails.
This season's diesel allowance for the drying line is $9,000.
The minimum diesel budget required to complete the run is $8,200.
$9,000 meets the minimum required budget of $8,200, so the diesel budget requirement passes.
probes.note: score max-over-premises bidirectional NLI of stale_hypothesis; compare to a corrected-sentence hypothesis built from correct_final_state. stale>=corrected => real contamination; corrected>stale => instrument false positive.
